interaction.prompt: You're F1. The opposing D just retrieved the puck in the strong-side corner. Skate to the spot that forces him to the wall, not up the middle.

feedback.right: Strong-side wall. You took away the inside lane and forced the carrier to the boards — F2 reads it and pinches.
feedback.wrong: If you charge straight at him, he goes either way. Take an angle: cut off the middle, push him to the boards.

tip: F1 forces a side. Never skate straight at the puck — angle so the carrier's only option is the wall.
why: Angling is the foundation of every forecheck. The mistake is going for the puck instead of the body line. Take the inside route away first; the wall does the rest of the work.
